meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize SaaS pricing to overcome project paralysis. A professional Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid MVP deployment.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing feature perfection. The 'can't finish' syndrome is usually a lack of market validation. By implementing aggressive SaaS pricing early, you force yourself to build only what users pay for. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing logic and Claude Code to iterate your MVP in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished dashboard, the third 'perfect' feature set abandoned in a forgotten repo. You’ve convinced yourself that one more refactor will make it launch-ready, but the truth is colder: you’re stuck in a loop of engineering perfectionism, not business execution. The project isn't stalling because of code; it's stalling because you haven't given yourself a commercial reason to ship.

True SaaS velocity comes from the intersection of pricing strategy and rapid deployment. When you attach a price tag to your MVP, the 'nice-to-haves' disappear, and the 'must-haves' become crystal clear. We are going to strip away the bloat, integrate a robust pricing engine, and use AI-driven workflows to get you from concept to a paid user in record time.

steps.title: Define Your Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Establish a three-tier pricing model based on user outcomes rather than features. This limits your MVP scope significantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema for a B2B productivity tool. Include Free, Pro ($29/mo), and Enterprise tiers. Focus on usage-based metrics that align with core value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a data structure for these pricing tiers and integrate a mock Stripe checkout flow.'

steps.title: Build the 'Pay-to-Access' Gate
steps.description: Force user authentication behind a payment wall to validate demand before building complex features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function for an Express/Next.js app that checks for an active Stripe subscription before allowing access to the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add middleware/auth.ts --context 'Ensure all /dashboard routes redirect to /pricing if no active subscription exists.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Scoping via AI
steps.description: Use AI to prune your roadmap. If a feature doesn't directly support the paid tier, delete it from the current sprint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current feature list and rank them by 'Revenue Potential' vs 'Development Effort'. Recommend which 3 to keep for a 2-week MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Remove all non-essential components from the codebase that are not part of the core MVP scope defined in the analysis.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhooks for Provisioning
steps.description: Automate the user experience. Once they pay, they should have instant access to their account features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler in TypeScript that updates the user record in Supabase upon successful payment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add api/webhooks/stripe.ts --context 'Use the latest Stripe SDK and handle checkout.session.completed events.'

steps.title: Deploy with Continuous Feedback Loops
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment and use logs to monitor what users actually click.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a lightweight analytics event tracker that logs every button click to a database for conversion analysis.

pro_tips: Never build a free tier that includes your core value proposition; it kills conversion.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by making your middle tier the obvious choice through feature omission in the base tier.
If you aren't getting emails from potential users within 48 hours of your landing page launch, kill the project and pivot.
Treat your codebase like a disposable asset; if it doesn't generate revenue, refactoring it is a waste of time.
Always set your first price point 20% higher than you feel comfortable with; it filters out low-quality, high-maintenance customers.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus entirely on top-of-funnel traffic. Do not touch the codebase for feature development for at least 30 days unless a paying user requests it.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting feature creep kill your business before it starts. Use these tools to tighten your scope, validate your pricing, and ship your MVP today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
